tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":57931,"title":"[Management Generator] Docs: Document extension resource detection with specific parent types","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57722) by `@live1206` ## What Changed PR #57722 fixed a bug in the management generator's resource detection logic to correctly handle **extension resources that target a specific parent resource type** (e.g., a resource that can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` rather than any arbitrary resource). The fix touched `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, and `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`. ## Gaps Found - The existing `docs/OperationContext.md` covers extension resources only for the **generic/wildcard scope** case (paths starting with `/{resourceUri}/providers/...`), but does not explain extension resources that specify a **specific parent resource type** via the TypeSpec `@armResourceOperations` scope, nor how the generator detects and differentiates between the two - There is no documentation explaining **how the generator detects whether an extension resource has a specific parent type vs. a wildcard scope**, and what generated code differs between the two cases (e.g., which extension class the resource's helper methods are generated on) - No CHANGELOG file exists in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to record this bug fix for users of the generator who may have been working around it 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` — **edit** Expand the existing **Extension Resources** subsection (under **Advanced Scenarios**) to cover the specific parent type case. Replace the current section: ````markdown ### Extension Resources Extension resources have a flexible scope (can extend any resource). Their contextual path starts with a single variable segment: ``` /{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The system handles these specially, treating `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's Id. ```` with: ````markdown ### Extension Resources Extension resources extend another Azure resource. The generator recognizes two sub-types: #### Wildcard-scope extension resources When an extension resource can extend **any** resource type, the operation paths use a single variable prefix: ``` /{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The system treats `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the extended resource's `Id`. Helper methods are added to the `ArmClient` mockable extension class so callers can invoke them from any `ArmResource`. #### Specific-parent extension resources When an extension resource is scoped to a **specific parent resource type** (for example, it can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`), the TypeSpec definition constrains the scope. The detected parent type determines which Azure scope class (e.g., `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, `TenantResource`, `ManagementGroupResource`, or the parent resource's own collection/resource class) receives the helper extension methods. The generator distinguishes these cases during resource detection by inspecting whether the extension resource's path prefix resolves to a known Azure scope path rather than a generic variable segment. When a specific parent is detected, the resource's `parentResourceId` in the code model is set to the concrete parent path, enabling the generator to place helper methods on the correct extension class. **Example:** An extension resource with parent type `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`: ``` /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The generator detects `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` as the specific parent and adds `GetExtension(string extensionName)` to the `MockableVirtualMachineResource` class, not the generic `ArmClient` extension. ```` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` — **create** Create a CHANGELOG file to track generator releases and bug fixes: ```markdown # Release History ## Unreleased ### Bug Fixes - Fixed detection of extension resources that target a specific parent resource type. Previously, extension resources with a constrained scope (e.g., restricted to `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`) were not correctly identified as having a specific parent, causing helper methods to be generated on the wrong extension class or missing entirely. ([#57722](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57722)) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No README snippet changes are needed for this developer-tool documentation update. The docs are prose-only markdown inside `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a generator tool README, not an end-user SDK README, so the standard template sections do not strictly apply. However, consider adding a **"Known Limitations / Notes"** or a link to the CHANGELOG once it exists. ### Step 4: Validate This is a documentation-only change (markdown files and a new CHANGELOG). No build or test commands are required beyond a basic review. However, to confirm the generator itself still works: 1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt && npm install` 2. `./eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` — re-generates all test projects; should complete with no errors 3. Review `git diff` on the generated test project files to confirm no regressions ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24129939236/agentic_workflow) · ● 472.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57931","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z"}

When a specific parent is detected, the resource's `parentResourceId` in the code model is set to the concrete parent path, enabling the generator to place helper methods on the correct extension class. **Example:** An extension resource with parent type `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`: ``` /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The generator detects `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` as the specific parent and adds `GetExtension(string extensionName)` to the `MockableVirtualMachineResource` class, not the generic `ArmClient` extension. ```` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` — **create** Create a CHANGELOG file to track generator releases and bug fixes: ```markdown # Release History ## Unreleased ### Bug Fixes - Fixed detection of extension resources that target a specific parent resource type. Previously, extension resources with a constrained scope (e.g., restricted to `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`) were not correctly identified as having a specific parent, causing helper methods to be generated on the wrong extension class or missing entirely. ([#57722](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57722)) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No README snippet changes are needed for this developer-tool documentation update. The docs are prose-only markdown inside `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a generator tool README, not an end-user SDK README, so the standard template sections do not strictly apply. However, consider adding a **"Known Limitations / Notes"** or a link to the CHANGELOG once it exists. ### Step 4: Validate This is a documentation-only change (markdown files and a new CHANGELOG). No build or test commands are required beyond a basic review. However, to confirm the generator itself still works: 1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt && npm install` 2. `./eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` — re-generates all test projects; should complete with no errors 3. Review `git diff` on the generated test project files to confirm no regressions ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24129939236/agentic_workflow) · ● 472.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57931","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z","updated_at":"2026-04-08T10:16:29Z"}

arguments.query: repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net management generator CodeGen label:customer-reported label:issue-addressed

arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the management generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt`) around extension resource detection — specifically, the missing coverage for extension resources targeting a specific parent resource type vs. a wildcard scope, and the absence of a CHANGELOG for the generator package.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` (management plane SDK generator)
**Affected API:** `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`; docs in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md`
**Scenarios:**
- Extension resources with a **wildcard scope** are documented in `OperationContext.md`, but extension resources with a **specific parent resource type** are not
- No explanation of how the generator distinguishes specific-parent vs. wildcard extension resources, or what generated code differs between the two cases
- No `CHANGELOG.md` in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to record the bug fix from PR #57722

**Root ask:** Expand `OperationContext.md` to cover specific-parent extension resources, and create a `CHANGELOG.md` for the management generator package.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only issue triggered by PR #57722 (commit `4ebb88df`), which fixed a bug in extension resource detection. The issue body contains a detailed implementation guide with exact markdown changes to apply.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #57722 to understand the specific detection logic added in `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, and `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`
2. Update `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` to add a new subsection on specific-parent extension resources as outlined in the implementation guide
3. Create `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` with an "Unreleased" section recording the PR #57722 bug fix
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — The issue is about the management plane code generator (`http-client-csharp-mgmt`), which generates `Azure.ResourceManager.*` SDKs
- **Service:** `CodeGen` — Issue is in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`, matching the convention that code generation issues in `eng/packages/` use the "CodeGen" service label
- **Confidence:** High — The package directory and generator name are unambiguous; CODEOWNERS confirms the `%CodeGen %Mgmt` ServiceLabel entry
</details>
